summary: Iteration 35 — multi IN/OUT punch backend verification. Created /app/backend/tests/test_iter35_multi_punch.py with 15 pytest cases covering employee /attendance/punch, admin /admin/attendance/approve-punch, /attendance/today, /attendance/summary (via _compute_day_hours), /admin/attendance/today aggregation, and /admin/payroll/run (via _compute_payroll_run). All 15/15 tests PASSED. The bug fix works exactly as specified: IN→OUT→IN→OUT produces 4 separate audit rows; double-IN, double-OUT and OUT-before-IN are rejected with 400 for both inside AND outside geofence paths and for the admin approve-punch path; /attendance/today returns all punches ordered by 'at' asc; /admin/attendance/today correctly aggregates first_in/last_out/total_hours/still_in/punches across multiple pairs (2h+2h = 4h, still_in=false when last punch is out, still_in=true when last punch is in); /admin/payroll/run sums total_hours=4.0 and punches=4 minutes=240 for two 2-hour blocks in one day. IN from outside geofence remains correctly blocked. Cleanup verified — 0 residual IT35_ rows in users, companies, user_sessions, attendance. Super admin credentials untouched.
critical_code_review_comments: Toggle idempotency in /api/attendance/punch is correctly implemented — today_recs are fetched once and reused for both geofence-open-IN check (line 3095) and inside-fence double-punch guard (lines 3109-3119). Consecutive last_kind logic is order-safe because sort('at', 1) is applied.
_compute_day_hours pairs punches correctly by walking chronologically, opening on IN and closing on OUT; still_in reflects an unclosed open_in — matches the review spec.

/admin/attendance/today builds per-user record buckets from a single company-scoped query and calls the shared _compute_day_hours — verified aggregation for punches=4, hours=4.0, still_in transitions correctly.

_compute_payroll_run walks each date bucket with its own has_in/open_in state — correctly re-opens on each new IN after an OUT (line 4049-4062), producing the expected 4h total for two 2-hour blocks the same day.
admin approve-punch guard replaced the old 'if kind==in and has_in' block-any-second-IN with last_kind toggle — verified admins can now record cycle-2 IN after a cycle-1 OUT.

Minor observation (not a bug for this iteration): /api/attendance/today caps at 50 records via to_list(50). If an employee legitimately has >50 punches in a single day this would silently truncate. Not in-scope for the current bug but worth flagging for a future iteration to bump to 200 to match the punch endpoint's own today_recs limit.

success_rate.frontend: not tested (backend-only iteration per review request)

seed_data_creation: Ephemeral only. Each test seeds companies (co_it35_*), users (user_it35_*), sessions (tk_it35_*), and attendance rows (att_it35_*) with the RUN_HEX marker in names. Session-scoped teardown removes all created rows. Confirmed 0 residual rows post-run. Super admin doc NOT touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_iter35_multi_punch.py as a regression suite for any future changes to /api/attendance/punch, /api/admin/attendance/approve-punch, _compute_day_hours, or _compute_payroll_run. It covers all shift-pairing edge cases including outside-geofence OUT paths and admin-approved multi-cycles. The suite uses office coords (13.0, 77.6) with a 200m fence — INSIDE=(13.0,77.6), OUTSIDE=(13.1,77.6) which is ~11km away.
rca_of_issue: Original bug: previously the /attendance/punch inside-geofence path had no toggle guard, and /admin/attendance/approve-punch used 'if kind=="in" and has_in' which blocked ANY second IN even after the first shift was closed with an OUT. Fix (validated): both endpoints now fetch today's records once, sort by 'at' asc, look at last_kind, and enforce toggle idempotency — reject IN when last_kind=='in', reject OUT when last_kind!='in'. Multiple full IN→OUT cycles per day are logged as separate audit rows and correctly aggregated by _compute_day_hours (used by /attendance/summary and /admin/attendance/today) and _compute_payroll_run (used by /admin/payroll/run).
